title: Categorized Free Recall with Closed-Loop Stimulation at Encoding (Encoding Classifier)
description: This dataset contains intracranial electrophysiology recordings and behavioral data from a categorized free recall task with real-time closed-loop brain stimulation during encoding. Participants studied semantically organized word lists, performed distractor tasks, and freely recalled items while a machine learning classifier trained on prior sessions predicted subsequent memory and triggered stimulation for low-confidence encoding events. The study investigates the causal role of neural activity patterns in memory formation across multiple clinical sites.
methods_description: Intracranial EEG recordings were acquired during a categorized free recall paradigm with 12-item word lists organized into 3 semantic categories (4 words per category). An L2 logistic regression classifier was trained on prior catFR1 sessions using spectral power features (Morlet wavelet transformation, 3-180 Hz, 8 log-spaced frequencies) extracted from the 0-1366 ms post-stimulus interval. Closed-loop stimulation (500 ms duration) was delivered to single electrodes when classifier output fell below participant-specific thresholds determined by Youden's J statistic. Recordings were acquired using Blackrock (250 nV units) or Medtronic (0.1 µV units) systems and referenced monopolarly or bipolarly according to participant-specific electrode montages.
